PATIENT HEALTH QUESTIONNAIRE 9-ITEM MR21 1 project (14 variables)
No description yet. Create Codebook/descriptions/patient_health_questionnaire_9_item.md to add one.
Variable Label Value Project In Projects
phq_1_pleasure Little interest or pleasure in doing things Not at all = 1, Several days =2, More than half the days = 3, Nearly every day = 4 MR21 MR21
phq_2_hopeless Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless Not at all = 1, Several days =2, More than half the days = 3, Nearly every day = 4 MR21 MR21
phq_3_sleep Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much Not at all = 1, Several days =2, More than half the days = 3, Nearly every day = 4 MR21 MR21
phq_4_tired Feeling tired or having little energy Not at all = 1, Several days =2, More than half the days = 3, Nearly every day = 4 MR21 MR21
phq_5_appetite Poor appetite or overeating Not at all = 1, Several days =2, More than half the days = 3, Nearly every day = 4 MR21 MR21
phq_6_feelbad Feeling bad about yourself or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down Not at all = 1, Several days =2, More than half the days = 3, Nearly every day = 4 MR21 MR21
phq_7_concentrate Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television Not at all = 1, Several days =2, More than half the days = 3, Nearly every day = 4 MR21 MR21
phq_8_moveslow Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed. Or the opposite being so fidgety or restless that oyu have been moving around a lot more than usual Not at all = 1, Several days =2, More than half the days = 3, Nearly every day = 4 MR21 MR21
phq_9_betteroffdead Thoughts that you would be better off dead, or of hurting yourself Not at all = 1, Several days =2, More than half the days = 3, Nearly every day = 4 MR21 MR21
phq_10_functioning If you checked off any problems, how difficult have these problems made it for you to do your work, take care of things at home, or get along with other people? Not difficult at all = 1, Somewhat difficult =2, Very difficult = 3, Extremely difficult = 4 MR21 MR21
phq_sum Sum of PHQ9 NA MR21 MR21
phq_sum_25 Sum of PHQ9 - 25% or less missing NA MR21 MR21
phq_dep_severity severity of PHQ depression minimal = 0, mild = 1, moderate = 2, moderately severe = 3, severe = 4 MR21 MR21
phq_dep_probable probable dperession based on PHQ9 score of 10 or greater No = 0, Yes = 1 MR21 MR21
PERCEPTIONS OF TEASING SCALE POWERED 1 project (19 variables)
No description yet. Create Codebook/descriptions/perceptions_of_teasing_scale.md to add one.
Variable Label Value Project In Projects
pots_timestamp Survey Timestamp NA POWERED POWERED
pots_instruc The following questions should be answered with respect to the period of time when you were growing up (ages 5-16). First, rate how often you think you have been the object of such behavior (using the scale provided, never to very often). Second, unless you responded never to a particular question, rate how upset you were by the teasing (not upset to very upset). NA POWERED POWERED
pots_make_fun_heavy People made fun of you because you were heavy. NA POWERED POWERED
pots_make_fun_heavy_upset How upset did this make you? NA POWERED POWERED
pots_jokes_heavy People made jokes about you being too heavy. NA POWERED POWERED
pots_jokes_heavy_upset How upset did this make you? NA POWERED POWERED
pots_attention If you are paying attention, select "sometimes" below. NA POWERED POWERED
pots_sports People laughed at you for trying out for sports because you were heavy NA POWERED POWERED
pots_sports_upset How upset did this make you? NA POWERED POWERED
pots_names People called you names like "fatso." NA POWERED POWERED
pots_names_upset How upset did this make you? NA POWERED POWERED
pots_point People pointed at you because you were overweight. NA POWERED POWERED
pots_point_upset How upset did this make you? NA POWERED POWERED
pots_laugh People laughed about your heaviness when you walk into a room alone. NA POWERED POWERED
pots_laugh_upset How upset did this make you? NA POWERED POWERED
pots_weight_who If you indicated that you have ever been teased for being heavy, please select who has teased you. NA POWERED POWERED
pots_who_other Who else has teased you for being heavy? NA POWERED POWERED
pots_weight_score Weight-Related Teasing Subscale NA POWERED POWERED
pots_weight_effect Weight Teasing Effect NA POWERED POWERED

POWERED WEIGHT HISTORY INTERVIEW POWERED 1 project (1096 variables)

Variable Label Value Project In Projects
interview_timestamp Survey Timestamp NA POWERED POWERED
desc1 ORIENTATION TO THE INTERVIEW: What we are going to do is a partially structured interview in which I will ask you about your medical and mental health history, including your eating disorder history, if applicable. Because we ask all participants these questions, some of these questions may not apply to you. PART 1: GROWTH CHART OVERVIEW I will begin the interview by asking you about your medical and mental health history at various points in your life. For this part of the interview, I will ask about medical and mental health conditions OTHER THAN an eating disorder. NA POWERED POWERED
surgnumb How many surgeries have you had? NA POWERED POWERED
surg1head Surgery 1 NA POWERED POWERED
surgreason What was the reason for the surgery? NA POWERED POWERED
surgage How old were you when you had this surgery? [Ask participant to make their best guess about the date. Try to remind the participant about seasons or events that might have been happening around the date of the surgery to jog their memory]. NA POWERED POWERED
surgeating How did the surgery impact your eating habits? [If it did not impact, write N/A] NA POWERED POWERED
surgeatingnormal When did your eating habits return to normal? NA POWERED POWERED
surgactivity How did the surgery impact your activity level? [If it did not impact, write N/A] NA POWERED POWERED
surgactivitynormal When did your activity habits return to normal? NA POWERED POWERED
surgcomp Please describe any complications from this surgery. [If their were no complications, write N/A] NA POWERED POWERED

rest_header WEIGHT LOSS/RESTRICTION NA POWERED POWERED
rest_yesno Has there ever been a time when you a) tried to limit the amount of food you ate to influence your weight, b) tried to exclude foods from your diet to influence your weight, and/or c) tried to follow rules (such as following a calorie limit) to influence your weight? NA POWERED POWERED
rest_startage How old were you when you first engaged in these behaviors to influence your weight? NA POWERED POWERED
rest_endage How old were you when you last engaged in these behaviors to influence your weight? NA POWERED POWERED
rest_directions Now, on a timeline, mark the age at which the participant first engaged in restriction and when they last engaged in restriction. Mark 6 month increments along the timeline. For each 6 month increment, assess whether the participant was engaging in clinical, subclinical, or no restriction. Criteria: Did you... 1. Deliberately try to limit the overall amount of food you ate? [No = the participant denies attempting to decrease the amount of food they were eating Subclinical = the participant attempted to reduce the amount of food/calories they were eating for a reason other than weight loss, was following a medically-prescribed eating plan in which caloric or food restriction was not drastic, or reduced the amount of food or calories they were eating, but the reduction was not drastic (< 300 calorie reduction from baseline) Clinical = the participant attempted to reduce the amount of food/calories they were eating drastically (> than 300 calorie reduction from baseline)] 2. Try to exclude food that you liked from your diet? [No = the participant denies excluding any food they liked from their diet Subclinical = the participant stopped eating 1-2 foods or excluded foods for reasons other than weight loss Clinical = the participant stopped eating more than 2 foods or excluded entire food groups with the goal of losing weight] 3. Try to follow rules related to food, such as calorie counting or cutting out food groups? [No = the participant denies following rules related to food Subclinical = the participant read food labels, considered the calories in food before eating it, or followed rules for medical reasons rather than weight loss Clinical = the participant followed a calorie limit, tracked food in an app, or followed rules that significantly limit the amount or types of food they could consume for the purpose of losing weight] 4. Skip meals? [No = the participant denies skipping meals (i.e., consistently ate 3 meals per day whenever possible) Subclinical = the participant skipped meals for reasons other than weight loss (not hungry in the morning, lack of time), skipped meals less than twice per week, or skipped meals to "save calories" for other meals] Clinical = the participant skipped meals more than 3 times per week for the purposes of weight loss] NA POWERED POWERED

uwl_1 Unintentional Weight Loss 1 NA POWERED POWERED
uwl_reason Reason for UWL? NA POWERED POWERED

purge_yesno Has there ever been a time where you made yourself vomit to control or influence your weight/shape? NA POWERED POWERED
purge_startage How old were you when you first made yourself vomit? NA POWERED POWERED
purge_endage How old were you when you last made yourself vomit? NA POWERED POWERED
purge_peak At the peak of your self-induced vomiting, how many times per month were you making yourself vomit? NA POWERED POWERED
purge_directions Starting with the first time the participant made themselves vomit, ask the participant how many times per month they were inducing vomiting. Mark this number on the timeline below. Repeat this for each 6-month increment until you reach the age at which the participant last made themselves vomit. For example, if the participant started this behavior at 14 years old, obtain the frequency of the behavior per month at 14 years old, 14.5 years old, 15 years old, etc. until you reach the age at which they last engaged in the behavior. NA POWERED POWERED

lax_yesno Has there ever been a time where you used laxatives to control or influence your weight/shape? NA POWERED POWERED
lax_startage How old were you when you first used laxatives for this purpose? NA POWERED POWERED
lax_endage How old were you the last time you used laxatives for this purpose? NA POWERED POWERED
lax_peak At the peak of your laxative use, how many times per month were you using them? NA POWERED POWERED
lax_directions Starting with the first time the participant used laxatives for this purpose, ask the participant how many times per month they were using laxatives. Mark this number on the timeline below. Repeat this for each 6-month increment until you reach the age at which the participant last used laxatives. For example, if the participant started this behavior at 14 years old, obtain the frequency of the behavior per month at 14 years old, 14.5 years old, 15 years old, etc. until you reach the age at which they last engaged in the behavior. For each age at which the participant was using laxatives, ask the participant to describe their average dose. NA POWERED POWERED

diuretic_directions [Starting with the first time the participant used diuretics for this purpose, ask the participant how many times per month they were using diuretics. sports_yesno Have you participated in physical activity such as marathon or triathlon training, powerlifting, fitness classes (i.e., indoor cycling), or any other activity you would consider rigorous, not for the purpose of weight loss? NA POWERED POWERED
sports_startage How old were you when you first exercised in this way? NA POWERED POWERED
sports_endage How old were you when you last exercised in this way? NA POWERED POWERED
sports_hours At the peak of this exercise, how many hours per week were you exercising? NA POWERED POWERED
exercise_info Additional info? NA POWERED POWERED
exercise_confident How confident are you in your answers about exercise? NA POWERED POWERED
binge_yesno Has there ever been a time when you have eaten what other people would regard as an unusually large amount of food given the circumstances within a short period of time (i.e., 2 hours)? NA POWERED POWERED
binge_control During these episodes, did you feel a sense of a loss of control? [If the participant clearly describes a loss of control, that they felt driven or compelled to eat, or that they felt unable to stop the episode once it began or to stop it from occurring, loss of control should be marked as present.] NA POWERED POWERED
binge_objective What did you typically eat during these episodes? [Assess whether what the participant describes is an objectively large amount of food given the circumstances. Take into account what is typical for participants in the same social group (i.e., age, sex, athletes, etc.) and social circumstances (i.e., holidays). Do not share your assessment with the participant.] Objective binge? NA POWERED POWERED

desc1_425648 ORIENTATION TO THE INTERVIEW: What we are going to do is a partially structured interview in which I will ask you about your medical and mental health history, including your eating disorder history, if applicable. Because we ask all participants these questions, some of these questions may not apply to you. PART 1: GROWTH CHART OVERVIEW I will begin the interview by asking you about your medical and mental health history at various points in your life. Prior to this interview you had filled out a survey asking about your medical history. I would like to go over those answers with you and ask a few follow up questions. NA POWERED POWERED

rest_header_c8edcf WEIGHT LOSS/RESTRICTION NA POWERED POWERED
rest_yesno_73eb16 Has there ever been a time when you a) tried to limit the amount of food you ate to influence your weight, b) tried to exclude foods from your diet to influence your weight, and/or c) tried to follow rules (such as following a calorie limit) to influence your weight? NA POWERED POWERED
rest_startage_a8df66 How old were you when you first engaged in these behaviors to influence your weight? NA POWERED POWERED
rest_endage_f44ba6 How old were you when you last engaged in these behaviors to influence your weight? NA POWERED POWERED
rest_directions_42bccf Now, on a timeline, mark the age at which the participant first engaged in restriction and when they last engaged in restriction. Mark 6 month increments along the timeline. For each 6 month increment, assess whether the participant was engaging in clinical, subclinical, or no restriction. Criteria: Did you... 1. Deliberately try to limit the overall amount of food you ate? [No = the participant denies attempting to decrease the amount of food they were eating Subclinical = the participant attempted to reduce the amount of food/calories they were eating for a reason other than weight loss, was following a medically-prescribed eating plan in which caloric or food restriction was not drastic, or reduced the amount of food or calories they were eating, but the reduction was not drastic (< 300 calorie reduction from baseline) Clinical = the participant attempted to reduce the amount of food/calories they were eating drastically (> than 300 calorie reduction from baseline)] 2. Try to exclude food that you liked from your diet? [No = the participant denies excluding any food they liked from their diet Subclinical = the participant stopped eating 1-2 foods or excluded foods for reasons other than weight loss Clinical = the participant stopped eating more than 2 foods or excluded entire food groups with the goal of losing weight] 3. Try to follow rules related to food, such as calorie counting or cutting out food groups? [No = the participant denies following rules related to food Subclinical = the participant read food labels, considered the calories in food before eating it, or followed rules for medical reasons rather than weight loss Clinical = the participant followed a calorie limit, tracked food in an app, or followed rules that significantly limit the amount or types of food they could consume for the purpose of losing weight] 4. Skip meals? [No = the participant denies skipping meals (i.e., consistently ate 3 meals per day whenever possible) Subclinical = the participant skipped meals for reasons other than weight loss (not hungry in the morning, lack of time), skipped meals less than twice per week, or skipped meals to "save calories" for other meals] Clinical = the participant skipped meals more than 3 times per week for the purposes of weight loss] NA POWERED POWERED

NA POWERED POWERED

pds_header Please answer the questions below to the best of your ability. NA POWERED POWERED

Variable Label Value Project In Projects
rcads_1_sad I feed sad or empty Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_2_poorly I worry when I think I have done poorly at something Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_3_afraid_home I would be afraid of being on my own at home Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_4_nofun Nothing is much fun for me anymore Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_5_family I worry that something awful will happen to someone in my family Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_6_crowded_places I am afriad of being in crowded places (like shopping centers, the movies, buses, busy playgrounds) Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_7_think I worry what other people think of me Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_8_sleep I have trouble sleeping Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_9_scared_sleep I feel scared if I have to sleep on my own Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_10_appetite I have problems with my appetite Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_11_dizzy I suddently become dizzy or faint when there is no reason for this Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_12_over I have to do somethings over and over again (like washing hands, cleaning, or putting things in a certain order) Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_13_noenergy I have no energy for things Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_14_tremble I suddenly start to tremble or shake when there is no reason for this Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_15_clearly I cannot think clearly Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_16_worthless I feel worthless Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_17_special_thoughts I have to think of special thoughts (like numbers or words) to stop bad things from happening Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_18_death I think about death Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_19_move I feel like I don't want to move Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_20_sudden_scared I worry that I will suddenly get a scared feeing when there is nothing to be afraid of Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_21_tired I am tired a lot Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_22_fool I feel afraid that I will make a fool of myself in front of people Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_23_right_way I have to do some things in just the right way to stop bad things from happening Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_24_restless I feel restless Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
recads_25_bad I worry that something bad will happen to me Never = 1, Sometimes = 2, Often = 3, Always = 4 MR21 MR21
rcads_sum RCADS SUM NA MR21 MR21
recads_anxiety_subscale RCADS ANXIETY SUBSCALE NA MR21 MR21
recads_depression_subscale RCADS DEPRESSION SUBSCALE NA MR21 MR21
SELFCOMPASSION SCALE POWERED 1 project (36 variables)
No description yet. Create Codebook/descriptions/selfcompassion_scale.md to add one.
Variable Label Value Project In Projects
selfcompassion_scale_timestamp Survey Timestamp NA POWERED POWERED
scs_description Please read each statement carefully before answering. Indicate how often you behave in the stated manner, using the following scale: Almost Never = 1 2 3 4 Almost Always = 5 NA POWERED POWERED
scs_disapproving I'm disapproving and judgmental about my own flaws and inadequacies. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_fixate When I'm feeling down I tend to obsess and fixate on everything that's wrong. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_everyone When things are going badly for me, I see the difficulties as part of life that everyone goes through. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_separate When I think about my inadequacies, it tends to make me feel more separate and cut off from the rest of the world. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_loving I try to be loving towards myself when I'm feeling emotional pain. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_inadequacy When I fail at something important to me I become consumed by feelings of inadequacy. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_downout When I'm down and out, I remind myself that there are lots of other people in the world feeling like I am. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_tough When times are really difficult, I tend to be tough on myself. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_balance When something upsets me I try to keep my emotions in balance. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_mostpeople When I feel inadequate in some way, I try to remind myself that feelings of inadequacy are shared by most people. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_intolerant I'm intolerant and impatient towards those aspects of my personality I don't like. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_caring When I'm going through a very hard time, I give myself the caring and tenderness I need. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_happier When I'm feeling down, I tend to feel like most other people are probably happier than I am. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_balanced When something painful happens I try to take a balanced view of the situation. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_failings I try to see my failings as part of the human condition. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_downonself When I see aspects of myself that I don't like, I get down on myself. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_perspective When I fail at something important to me I try to keep things in perspective. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_struggling When I'm really struggling, I tend to feel like other people must be having an easier time of it. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_suffering I'm kind to myself when I'm experiencing suffering. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_carriedaway When something upsets me I get carried away with my feelings. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_coldheart I can be a bit cold-hearted towards myself when I'm experiencing suffering. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_curiosity When I'm feeling down I try to approach my feelings with curiosity and openness. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_tolerant I'm tolerant of my own flaws and inadequacies. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_attention Select 'Almost Never' if you are paying attention. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_proportion When something painful happens I tend to blow the incident out of proportion. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_alone When I fail at something that's important to me, I tend to feel alone in my failure. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_understanding I try to be understanding and patient towards those aspects of my personality I don't like. NA POWERED POWERED
scs_self_kindness_score Self Kindness Scale Total NA POWERED POWERED
scs_selfjudgment_score Self Judgment Scale Total (Reverse Scored) NA POWERED POWERED
scs_commonhumanity_score Common Humanity Scale Total NA POWERED POWERED
scs_isolation_score Isolation Scale Total (Reverse Scored) NA POWERED POWERED
scs_mindfulness_score Mindfulness Scale Total NA POWERED POWERED
scs_overidentified_score Over-Identified Scale Total (Reverse Scored) NA POWERED POWERED
scs_total_score Total Self Compassion Score NA POWERED POWERED

sataq_timestamp Survey Timestamp NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_1_athletic It is important for me to look athletic. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_2_muscular I think a lot about looking muscular. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_3_thin I want my body to look very thin. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_4_no_fat I want my body to look like it has little fat. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_5_look_thin I think a lot about looking thin. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_6_look_athletic I spend a lot of time doing things to look more athletic. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_7_think_athletic I think a lot about looking athletic NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_8_look_lean I want my body to look very lean. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_9_little_fat I think a lot about having very little body fat. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_10_look_muscular I spend a lot of time doing things to look more muscular. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_11_family_thin I feel pressure from family members to look thinner. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_12_family_improve I feel pressure from family members to improve my appearance. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_13_family_fat Family members encourage me to decrease my level of body fat. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_14_family_shape Family members encourage me to get in better shape. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_15_peer_thin My peers encourage me to get thinner. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_16_peer_improve I feel pressure from my peers to improve my appearance. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_17_peer_shape I feel pressure from my peers to look in better shape. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_18_peer_body_fat I get pressure from my peers to decrease my level of body fat. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_19_media_shape I feel pressure from the media to look in better shape. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_20_media_thin I feel pressure from the media to look thinner. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_ac_1 Select "Definitely Disagree" if you are paying attention. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_21_media_look I feel pressure from the media to improve my appearance. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_22_media_fat I feel pressure from the media to decrease my level of body fat. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_doc_shape I feel pressure from healthcare providers to look in better shape. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_doc_thin I feel pressure from the healthcare providers to look thinner. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_doc_look I feel pressure from healthcare providers to improve my appearance. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_doc_fat I feel pressure from the healthcare providers to decrease my level of body fat. NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_average SATAQ Average Score NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_thin_ideal SATAQ Thin Ideal NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_athletic_idea SATAQ athletic average NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_parental_pressure SATAQ Parental Pressure Average NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_peer_pressure SATAQ peer pressure average NA POWERED POWERED
sataq_media_pressure SATAQ media pressure average NA POWERED POWERED

Variable Label Value Project In Projects
spsrq_1_illegal Do you often refrain from doing something because you are afraid of it being illegal? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_2_money_positive Does the good prospect of obtaining money motivate you strongly to do something? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_3_certain_obtain prefer not to ask for something when you are not sure you will obtain it? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_4_others_value Are you frequently encouraged to act by the possibility of being va ... r friends or with your family? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_5_fear_unexpected Are you often afraid of new or unexpected situations? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_6_physical_attract Do you often meet people that you find physically attractive? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_7_telephone Is it difficult for you to telephone someone you do not know? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_8_drug_pleasant Do you like to take some drugs because of the pleasure you get from them? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_9_right Do you often renounce your rights when you know you can avoid a qua ... h a person or an organization? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_10_praise Do you often do things to be praised? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_11_punish_concern As a child, were you trouble by punishments at home or in school? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_12_attention Do you like being the center of attention at a party or a social meeting? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_13_failure In tasks that you are not prepared for, do you attach great importance to the possibility of failure? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_14_good_image Do you spend a lot of your time on obtaining a good image? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_15_discourage Are you easily discouraged in difficult situations? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_16_affection Do you need people to show their affection for you all the time? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_17_shy Are you a shy person? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_18_intelligent When you are in a group, do you try to make your opinions the most intelligent or the funniest? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_19_embarassed Whenever possible, do you avoid demonstrating your skills for fear of being embarrassed? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_20_pick_up Do you often take the opportunity to pick up people you find attractive? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_21_topic When you are with a group, do you have difficulties selecting a good topic to talk about? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_22_approval As a child, did you do a lot of things to get people鈥檚 approval? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_23_fall_asleep Is it often difficult for you to fall asleep when you think about things you have done or must do? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_24_unfair Does the possibility of social advancement, move you to action, even if this involves not playing fair? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_25_meal_complain Do you think a lot before complaining in a restaurant if your meal is not well prepared? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsqr_26_immediate_gain Do you generally give preferences to those activities that imply an immediate gain? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_27_wrong_change Would you be bothered if you had to return to a store when you noticed you were given the wrong change? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_28_forbidden Do you often have trouble resisting the temptation of doing forbidden things? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_29_unknown_place Whenever you can, do you avoid going to unknown places? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_30_win Do you like to compete and do everything you can to win? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_31_worry Are you often worried by things that you said or did? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_32_pleasant_relate Is it easy for you to associate tastes and smells to very pleasant events? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_33_salary_increase Would it be difficult for you to ask your boss for a raise (salary increase)? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_34_pleasant_remind Are there a large number of objects or sensations that remind you of pleasant events? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_35_public_speak Do you generally try to avoid speaking in public? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_36_slot_machine When you start to play with a slot machine, is it often difficult for you to stop? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_37_do_more Do you, on a regular basis, think that you could do more things if it was not for your insecurity or fear? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_38_quick_gain Do you sometimes do things for quick gains? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_39_fear Comparing yourself to people you know, are you afraid of many things? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_40_stray_off Does your attention easily stray from your work in the presence of an attractive stranger? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_41_inhibition Do you often find yourself worrying about things to the extent that performance in intellectual abilities is impaired? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_42_money_risk Are you interested in money to the point of being able to do risky jobs? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_43_rejection Do you often refrain from doing something you like in order not to be rejected or disapproved of by others? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_44_competitive Do you like to put competitive ingredients in all of your activities? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_45_threat_attend Generally, do you pay more attention to threats than to pleasant events? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_46_social_power Would you like to be a socially powerful person? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_47_fear_embarrased Do you often refrain from doing something because of your fear of being embarrassed? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_48_physical_danger Do you like displaying your physical abilities even though this may involve danger? 1=yes, 0=no MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_reward_sum reward scale score NA MAXED MAXEDMR21
spsrq_punishiment_sum punishment scale score NA MAXED MAXEDMR21

Variable Label Value Project In Projects
srhi_1_frequently Habitual eating is something I do frequently. 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_2_automatically Habitual eating is something I do automatically. 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_3_consciously Habitual eating is something I do without having to consciously remember. 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_4_weird Habitual eating is something that makes me feel weird if I do not do it. 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_5_without_thinking Habitual eating is something I do without thinking. 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_6_require_effort Habitual eating is something that would require effort not to do it. 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_7_routine Habitual eating is something that belongs to my (daily, weekly, monthly) routine. 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_8_before_realizing Habitual eating is something I start doing before I realize I'm doing it 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_9_hard Habitual eating is something I would find hard not to do. 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_10_no_need Habitual eating is something I have no need to think about doing. 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_11_typically_me Habitual eating is something that's typically "me". 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_12_long_time Habitual is something I have been doing for a long time. 0 = Strongly disagree | 1 = Disagree | 2 = Neutral | 3 = Agree | 4 = Strongly Agree MAXED MAXEDMR21
srhi_mean_raw raw mean of self report habit index NA MAXED MAXED

milk 9. Milkshake: (Document food intolerances/preferred flavor) {milk_flavor} starting weight of milkshake: {milk_start_weight} end weight of milkshake: {milk_end_weight} Percent of milkshake drank: {milk_drank_percent_a} NA MAXED MAXED
milk_flavor Milkshake flavor NA MAXED MAXED
milk_start_weight Starting weight of milk shake (grams): NA MAXED MAXED
milk_end_weight End weight of milkshake (grams): NA MAXED MAXED
milk_drank_percent_a Percent of Milkshake Drank if([milk_end_weight] <> "" and [milk_start_weight] <> "", 100 - (([milk_end_weight] / [milk_start_weight]) * 100), "") MAXED MAXEDMR21
studya_hr_info <div class="rich-text-field-label"><p><span style="font-weight: normal;">Seat Height: {studya_seat_height} </span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: normal;">Age: {studya_age}</span></p> <p>Age-Predicted Max HR:<span style="font-weight: normal;"> {studya_max_hr}</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: normal;">{studya_40_hr} (40%)   {studya_55_hr} (55%) </span></p> <p>This is self-paced but particpants can go above the Moderate Intensity level: <span style="font-weight: normal;">can't go above 80%: {studya_80_hr} (80%)</span>  </p></div> NA MAXED MAXEDMR21
studya_seat_height Seat Height NA MAXED MAXEDMR21
studya_age Age: NA MAXED MAXEDMR21
studya_max_hr Age-Predicted Max HR: 220-Age = if([studya_age] <> "", 220 - [studya_age], "") MAXED MAXEDMR21
studya_40_hr 40% [studya_max_hr]*.40 MAXED MAXEDMR21
studya_55_hr 55% [studya_max_hr]*.55 MAXED MAXEDMR21
studya_80_hr 80% NA MAXED MAXEDMR21

studya_hr_info <div class="rich-text-field-label"><p><span style="font-weight: normal;">Seat Height: {studya_seat_height} </span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: normal;">Age: {studya_age}</span></p> <p>Age-Predicted Max HR:<span style="font-weight: normal;"> {studya_max_hr}</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: normal;">{studya_40_hr} (40%)   {studya_55_hr} (55%) </span></p> <p>This is self-paced but particpants can go above the Moderate Intensity level: <span style="font-weight: normal;">can't go above 80%: {studya_80_hr} (80%)</span>  </p></div> NA MR21 MAXEDMR21
studya_seat_height Seat Height NA MR21 MAXEDMR21

studya_max_hr Age-Predicted Max HR: 220-Age = if([studya_age] <> "", 220 - [studya_age], "") MR21 MAXEDMR21
studya_40_hr 40% [studya_max_hr]*.40 MR21 MAXEDMR21
studya_55_hr 55% [studya_max_hr]*.55 MR21 MAXEDMR21
studya_80_hr 80% [studya_max_hr]*.80 MR21 MAXEDMR21

studya_hr_info <div class="rich-text-field-label"><p><span style="font-weight: normal;">Seat Height: {studya_seat_height} </span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: normal;">Age: {studya_age}</span></p> <p>Age-Predicted Max HR:<span style="font-weight: normal;"> {studya_max_hr}</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: normal;">{studya_40_hr} (40%)   {studya_55_hr} (55%) </span></p> <p>This is self-paced but particpants can go above the Moderate Intensity level: <span style="font-weight: normal;">can't go above 80%: {studya_80_hr} (80%)</span>  </p></div> NA MR21 MAXEDMR21
studya_seat_height Seat Height NA MR21 MAXEDMR21
studya_age Age: NA MR21 MAXEDMR21
studya_max_hr Age-Predicted Max HR: 220-Age = if([studya_age] <> "", 220 - [studya_age], "") MR21 MAXEDMR21
studya_40_hr 40% [studya_max_hr]*.40 MR21 MAXEDMR21
studya_55_hr 55% [studya_max_hr]*.55 MR21 MAXEDMR21
studya_80_hr 80% [studya_max_hr]*.80 MR21 MAXEDMR21

studyb_hr_info <div class="rich-text-field-label"><p>Warm-up should begin at a HR which approximates 40% MHR and end near 55% MHR (maintain 60-70 revolutions/min).</p> <p>Note regarding HR moderate-intensity session a try to get HR up closer to 55% MHR by the end of the work out.</p> <p>Note regarding watts moderate-intensity session a start adjusting watts right around 1-min mark (increase around 5-10 watts/minute for first 3 minutes and then 10-20 watts/minute during minutes 4 and 5 of warm-up).</p> <p> </p> <p>Age-Predicted Max HR: 220-Age= {studyb_max_hr}</p> <p>  {studyb_hr_40}  (40%)     {studyb_hr_55} (55%) Warm-Up/Cool off Lasts 5 minutes</p> <p>Moderate Intensity Exercise Session: Stay between 70-75% MHR (while maintaining 60-70 rpm)</p> <p>{studyb_hr_70} (70%) {studyb_hr_75} (75%)</p></div> NA MAXED MAXEDMR21
studyb_max_hr Age-Predicted Max HR: 220-Age = if([studyb_age] <> "", 220 - [studyb_age], "") MAXED MAXEDMR21

recordwandh_b <div class="rich-text-field-label"><table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 27.5813%; height: 85px; border-style: outset;" border="1"> <tbody> <tr style="height: 42.5px;"> <td style="width: 33.4998%; height: 42.5px;">Weight (lbs): </td> <td style="width: 66.5083%; height: 42.5px;">{weight_1_b}</td> </tr> <tr style="height: 42.5px;"> <td style="width: 33.4998%; height: 42.5px;">Height (ft): </td> <td style="width: 66.5083%; height: 42.5px;">{height_1_b}</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p> </p> <table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 27.5813%; height: 67.5px; border-style: outset;" border="1"> <tbody> <tr style="height: 42.5px;"> <td style="width: 32.9816%; height: 42.5px;">Weight (lbs): </td> <td style="width: 67.0264%; height: 42.5px;">{weight_2_b}</td> </tr> <tr style="height: 25px;"> <td style="width: 32.9816%; height: 25px;">Height (ft): </td> <td style="width: 67.0264%; height: 25px;">{height_2_b}</td> </tr> </tbody> </table></div> NA MAXED MAXEDMR21

studyb_info <div class="rich-text-field-label"><table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%;" border="1"> <tbody> <tr> <td style="width: 33.2854%;">Date: {studyb_date}</td> <td style="width: 33.2854%;">Visit Start Time: {studyb_time_start}</td> <td style="width: 33.2877%;">Visit End Time: {studyb_time_end}</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p style="text-align: left;">1. Consumed food or drinks other than water in past two hours? {studyb_consumed_food}</p> <p style="text-align: left;">2.  Exercised in the past 24 hours? {studyb_24h_ex}</p> <p style="text-align: left;">3. Preferred Snack: {studyb_snack}</p> <p>4. Collect blood pressure and HR:</p> <table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 21.6407%; height: 50px;" border="1"> <tbody> <tr style="height: 25px;"> <td style="width: 16.087%; height: 25px;">HR: </td> <td style="width: 83.913%; height: 25px;">{studyb_bl_hr}</td> </tr> <tr style="height: 25px;"> <td style="width: 16.087%; height: 25px;">BP: </td> <td style="width: 83.913%; height: 25px;">{studyb_bl_bp}</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p style="text-align: left;">                                                                 </p></div> NA MAXED MAXEDMR21

studyb_hr_info <div class="rich-text-field-label"><p>Warm-up should begin at a HR which approximates 40% MHR and end near 55% MHR (maintain 60-70 revolutions/min).</p> <p>Note regarding HR moderate-intensity session a try to get HR up closer to 55% MHR by the end of the work out.</p> <p>Note regarding watts moderate-intensity session a start adjusting watts right around 1-min mark (increase around 5-10 watts/minute for first 3 minutes and then 10-20 watts/minute during minutes 4 and 5 of warm-up).</p> <p> </p> <p>Age-Predicted Max HR: 220-Age= {studyb_max_hr}</p> <p>  {studyb_hr_40}  (40%)     {studyb_hr_55} (55%) Warm-Up/Cool off Lasts 5 minutes</p> <p>Moderate Intensity Exercise Session: Stay between 70-75% MHR (while maintaining 60-70 rpm)</p> <p>{studyb_hr_70} (70%) {studyb_hr_75} (75%)</p></div> NA MAXED MAXEDMR21
studyb_max_hr Age-Predicted Max HR: 220-Age = if([studyb_age] <> "", 220 - [studyb_age], "") MAXED MAXEDMR21
studyb_age Age: NA MAXED MAXEDMR21

Preferred Snack: {studyb_snack}</p> <p>4. Collect blood pressure and HR:</p> <table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 21.6407%; height: 50px;" border="1"> <tbody> <tr style="height: 25px;"> <td style="width: 16.087%; height: 25px;">HR: </td> <td style="width: 83.913%; height: 25px;">{studyb_bl_hr}</td> </tr> <tr style="height: 25px;"> <td style="width: 16.087%; height: 25px;">BP: </td> <td style="width: 83.913%; height: 25px;">{studyb_bl_bp}</td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p style="text-align: left;">                                                                 </p></div> NA MAXED MAXEDMR21

studyb_hr_info <div class="rich-text-field-label"><p>Warm-up should begin at a HR which approximates 40% MHR and end near 55% MHR (maintain 60-70 revolutions/min).</p> <p>Note regarding HR moderate-intensity session a try to get HR up closer to 55% MHR by the end of the work out.</p> <p>Note regarding watts moderate-intensity session a start adjusting watts right around 1-min mark (increase around 5-10 watts/minute for first 3 minutes and then 10-20 watts/minute during minutes 4 and 5 of warm-up).</p> <p> </p> <p>Age-Predicted Max HR: 220-Age= {studyb_max_hr}</p> <p>  {studyb_hr_40}  (40%)     {studyb_hr_55} (55%) Warm-Up/Cool off Lasts 5 minutes</p> <p>Moderate Intensity Exercise Session: Stay between 70-75% MHR (while maintaining 60-70 rpm)</p> <p>{studyb_hr_70} (70%) {studyb_hr_75} (75%)</p></div> NA MAXED MAXEDMR21
studyb_max_hr Age-Predicted Max HR: 220-Age = if([studyb_age] <> "", 220 - [studyb_age], "") MAXED MAXEDMR21
studyb_age Age: NA MAXED MAXEDMR21

studyb_hr_info <div class="rich-text-field-label"><p>Warm-up should begin at a HR which approximates 40% MHR and end near 55% MHR (maintain 60-70 revolutions/min).</p> <p>Note regarding HR moderate-intensity session a try to get HR up closer to 55% MHR by the end of the work out.</p> <p>Note regarding watts moderate-intensity session a start adjusting watts right around 1-min mark (increase around 5-10 watts/minute for first 3 minutes and then 10-20 watts/minute during minutes 4 and 5 of warm-up).</p> <p> </p> <p>Age-Predicted Max HR: 220-Age= {studyb_max_hr}</p> <p>  {studyb_hr_40}  (40%)     {studyb_hr_55} (55%) Warm-Up/Cool off Lasts 5 minutes</p> <p>Moderate Intensity Exercise Session: Stay between 70-75% MHR (while maintaining 60-70 rpm)</p> <p>{studyb_hr_70} (70%) {studyb_hr_75} (75%)</p></div> NA MR21 MAXEDMR21
studyb_age Age: NA MR21 MAXEDMR21
studyb_max_hr Age-Predicted Max HR: 220-Age = if([studyb_age] <> "", 220 - [studyb_age], "") MR21 MAXEDMR21
studyb_hr_40 40% [studyb_max_hr]*.40 MR21 MAXEDMR21
studyb_hr_55 55% [studyb_max_hr]*.55 MR21 MAXEDMR21
studyb_hr_70 70 [studyb_max_hr]*.70 MR21 MAXEDMR21
studyb_hr_75 75% [studyb_max_hr]*.75 MR21 MAXEDMR21

TANNER STAGES MAXEDMR21POWERED All 3 projects (8 variables)
No description yet. Create Codebook/descriptions/tanner_stages.md to add one.
Variable Label Value Project In Projects
tanner_sex Please indicate your biological sex. 1, Female | 2, Male | 3, Others/I choose not to answer MAXED MAXED
tanner_photo NA NA MAXED MAXEDMR21POWERED
tanner_hair Please indicate your pubic hair level. 1, No pubic hair (stage 1) | 2, Initial growth of long pubic hairs. Straight, without curls and light color (stage 2) | 3, Pubic hair is more widespread. Hair is darker and curls may have appeared (stage 3) | 4, More dense hair growth with curls and dark hair. (stage 4) | 5, Adult hair growth. Dense, curly hair extending towards the inner thighs (stage 5) MAXED MAXEDMR21POWERED
tanner_breast Please indicate your breast development level (for female only). 1, Small nipples, No breast (stage 1) | 2, Breast and nipples have just started to grow. The areola has become larger. Breast tissue bud feels firm and behind the nipple (stage 2) | 3, Breast and nipples have grown additionally. The areola has become darker. The breast tissue bud is larger (stage 3) | 4, Nipples and areolas are elevated and form an edge towards the breast, The breast has also grown a little larger (stage 4) | 5, Fully developed breast. Nipples are protruding, and the edge between areola and breast has disappeared (stage 5) | 6, Not applicable MAXED MAXEDMR21POWERED
tanner_hair_male Please indicate your pubic hair level. 1, No pubic hair (stage 1) | 2, Few hairs around the root of the penis. The hairs are straight, without curls, and of light color (stage 2). | 3, Hairs are darker and curlier and still sparse, mostly located at the penis root (stage 3). | 4, More dense, curly, and dark hair. The hair growth is reaching the inner thighs (stage 4). | 5, Pubic hair extends upwards to the umbilicus. It is dense and curly (stage 5). | 6, Not applicable MAXED MAXED
tanner_external_genetalia Please indicate your external genitalia scale (for male only). 1, No signs of puberty. Scrotum, testes, and penis as in childhood (stage 1) | 2, Initial growth of scrotum and testes. The skin on the scrotum has become redder, thinner, and more wrinkled. The penis may have grown a little in length (stage 2) | 3, The penis has now grown in length. Scrotum and testes have grown. The skin of the scrotum has become darker and more wrinkled (stage 3) | 4, The penis has grown in both length and width. The head of the penis has become larger. The scrotum and testes have grown. (stage 4) | 5, Penis and scrotum as an adult (stage 5) | 6, Not applicable MAXED MAXED
tanner_photo NA NA MAXED MAXEDMR21POWERED
tanner_photo NA NA MAXED MAXEDMR21POWERED
tanner_age What is your age? 1, 16 | 2, 17 | 3, 18 | 4, 19 | 5, 20 | 6, 21 | 7, 22 | 8, 23 | 9, 24 | 10, 25 MR21 MR21
tanner_photo NA NA MR21 MAXEDMR21POWERED
tanner_hair Please indicate your pubic hair level. 1, No pubic hair (stage 1) | 2, Initial growth of long pubic hairs. Straight, without curls and light color (stage 2) | 3, Pubic hair is more widespread. Hair is darker and curls may have appeared (stage 3) | 4, More dense hair growth with curls and dark hair. (stage 4) | 5, Adult hair growth. Dense, curly hair extending towards the inner thighs (stage 5) MR21 MAXEDMR21POWERED
tanner_breast Please indicate your breast development level. 1, Small nipples, No breast (stage 1) | 2, Breast and nipples have just started to grow. The areola has become larger. Breast tissue bud feels firm and behind the nipple (stage 2) | 3, Breast and nipples have grown additionally. The areola has become darker. The breast tissue bud is larger (stage 3) | 4, Nipples and areolas are elevated and form an edge towards the breast, The breast has also grown a little larger (stage 4) | 5, Fully developed breast. Nipples are protruding, and the edge between areola and breast has disappeared (stage 5) | 6, Not applicable MR21 MAXEDMR21POWERED

YBOCS MAXED 1 project (17 variables)
No description yet. Create Codebook/descriptions/ybocs.md to add one.
Variable Label Value Project In Projects
ybocs_1_time_thoughts How much time do you spend on obsessive thoughts? 0 = none | 1 = 0-1 hrs/day | 2 = 1-3 hrs/day | 3 = 3-8 hrs/day | 4 = More than 8 hrs/day MAXED MAXED
ybocs_2_thoughts_interfere How much do your obsessive thoughts interfere with your personal, social, or work life? 0 = none | 1 = mild | 2 = definite but manageable | 3 = substantial interference | 4 = severe MAXED MAXED
ybocs_3_thoughts_distress How much do your obsessive thoughts distress you? 0 = none | 1 = little | 2 = moderate but manageable | 3 = severe | 4 = nearly constant, disabling MAXED MAXED
ybocs_4_resist_obsessions How hard do you try to resist your obsessions? 0 = Always try | 1 = Try much of the time | 2 = Try some of the time | 3 = Rarely try | 3 = Often yield | 4 = Never try | 4 = Completely yield MAXED MAXED
ybocs_5_control_obsessions How much control do you have over your obsessive thoughts? 0 = complete control | 1 = Much control | 2 = Some control | 3 = Little control | 4 = No control MAXED MAXED
ybocs_6_time_compulsions How much time do you spend performing compulsive behaviors? 0 = none | 1 = 0-1 hrs/day | 2 = 1-3 hrs/day | 3 = 3-8 hrs/day | 4 = More than 8 hrs/day MAXED MAXED
ybocs_7_compulsions_interfere How much do your compulsive behaviors interfere with your personal, social, or work life? 0 = none | 1 = mild | 2 = definite but manageable | 3 = substantial interference | 4 = severe MAXED MAXED
ybocs_8_anxious How anxious would you feel if you were prevented from performing your compulsive behaviors? 0 = none | 1 = little | 2 = moderate but manageable | 3 = severe | 4 = nearly constant, disabling MAXED MAXED
ybocs_9_resist_compulsions How hard do you try to resist your compulsive behaviors? 0 = Always try | 1 = Try much of the time | 2 = Try some of the time | 3 = Rarely try, often yield | 4 = Never try, completely yield MAXED MAXED
ybocs_10_control_compulsions How much control do you have over your compulsive behaviors? 0 = complete control | 1 = Much control | 2 = Some control | 3 = Little control | 4 = No control MAXED MAXED
ybocs_obsessions_raw raw sum score for obsessions NA MAXED MAXED
ybocs_compulsions_raw raw sum score for compulsions NA MAXED MAXED
ybocs_obsessions_sum sum score for obsessions (< 25% missing) NA MAXED MAXED
ybocs_compulsions_sum sum score for compulsions (< 25% missing) NA MAXED MAXED
ybocs_total_score_raw raw YBOCS total score NA MAXED MAXED
ybocs_total_score YBOCS total score (< 25% missing) NA MAXED MAXED
ybocs_severity severity index for obsessions 0 = subclinical | 1 = mild | 2 = moderate | 3 = severe | 4 = extreme MAXED MAXED
